appDescription: Transform your mental wellness journey into a gamified adventure. Conquer OCD and anxiety with NeuroQuest.

dashboard.overviewDescription: A quick summary of your latest state and what to focus on next.
dashboard.activeMissionHint: Generate or create a mission to keep your ERP work moving.
dashboard.summaryNoCheckIn: Complete your first mental check-in to unlock a personalized summary here.
dashboard.recentActivityHint: Complete actions in modules to see the latest useful movement here.
dashboard.crisisBannerTitle: You need an immediate support path
dashboard.crisisBannerDescription: Your latest check-in was marked severe. Open crisis support to view emergency lines and care centers in Medellin.

summaryBySeverity.healthy: Your latest check-in looks stable. Keep reinforcing the routines that are working.
summaryBySeverity.mild: Your latest check-in shows mild strain. Stay consistent with grounding and short recovery habits.
summaryBySeverity.moderate: Your latest check-in shows moderate discomfort. Focus on one active mission and lower avoidant behaviors.
summaryBySeverity.severe: Your latest check-in suggests high distress. Prioritize support, stabilization, and small next actions.

dashboard.nextMissionDescription: Our AI has analyzed your recent activity to create a new challenge for you.

upcoming.challengesDescription: This route is outside the current MVP scope. The core tracking and therapy flows are already functional.

upcoming.readyDescription: These are the real routes and flows available in the MVP today.

readyItems.dashboard: Dashboard connected to Firestore with current state, XP, level, and active mission.
readyItems.checkIn: Mental Check-In with real history persistence and denormalized user updates.
readyItems.progress: Progress with user metrics, trend lines, and latest records.
readyItems.observer: Observer with saved thoughts, analysis, and pattern tracking.
readyItems.therapist: Therapist with real patient lists and per-patient clinical detail.

hero.badge: A mental-health tracking platform with clinical structure
hero.title: Your mind, supported every day
hero.description: Understand how you feel, record what you think, and follow your progress with clarity.

previewTabs.label: Dashboard
previewTabs.title: Immediate visibility into current state
previewTabs.body: Users see their latest check-in, level, XP, active mission, and a short summary that clarifies the next step.

hero.followUpTitle: Clinical state remains legible between sessions.

highlights.title: Clinical flow with real closure

highlights.title: Calm clinical motion

proof.title: Clinical visibility, continuity, and flow closure inside one system.

logoExperience.title: An organic visual signature for a contemporary clinical platform.
logoExperience.description: This animated mark uses WebGL and soft deformation to express movement, adaptation, and continuous follow-up without breaking the product's calm visual language.
logoExperience.caption: Subtle interaction: on hover, the shape slightly increases its distortion.

why.title: NeuroQuest supports you between sessions so you do not lose the thread of your process.
why.description: NeuroQuest helps you make sense of your mental state in a simple and continuous way. It turns what you feel and think into clearer information so you can understand yourself better and move forward step by step. It does not replace your therapist. It helps you arrive better prepared and with more clarity.

cards.title: Clear current state
cards.description: See how you are today without getting lost in confusing data or crowded screens.

cards.title: Progress over time
cards.description: Follow changes, gains, and variations across the process with real context.

cards.title: Patterns and signals
cards.description: Identify repeated thoughts, important signals, and moments worth reviewing.

why.caseReadingTitle: One view integrates recent severity, observer, progress, and active tasks.

modules.title: Clear information to understand what is happening to you
modules.description: NeuroQuest organizes your information so you can see it more clearly and act in time.
cards: Understand how you feel today with a simple structured view.
See changes and real evolution without losing continuity.
Record thoughts and context to recognize recurring patterns.
Identify moments when it is worth acting or asking for support.

journey.title: A simple flow to understand yourself better, step by step.

steps.title: Assess yourself in minutes
steps.description: Answer a simple check-in to understand how you are today.

steps.title: Record what is on your mind
steps.description: Save thoughts, situations, and meaningful emotions.

steps.title: Visualize your progress
steps.description: See how you change over time without losing context.

steps.title: Share with your therapist
steps.description: Your process becomes clearer and more useful in each session.

therapistDemo.title: A clearer way to understand each patient between sessions.
therapistDemo.description: NeuroQuest offers a clearer view of the patient process to support clinical decision-making.
therapistDemo.panelSummary: Access real patient evolution, emotional context, and visible signals to intervene with more clarity.
therapistDemo.observerBody: Trigger: contamination. Compulsion urge: high. Recent situation documented.
therapistDemo.activityBody: Active ERP mission, longitudinal progress available, and basic risk visible.
timeline: Real follow-up of changes and continuity between sessions.

audiences.title: One platform, with useful views for patients and professionals.
audiences.patientIntro: A clearer experience to understand mental state, sustain adherence, and lower follow-up friction.
audiences.therapistIntro: A faster clinical reading of what happened between sessions, without reconstructing everything manually.
patientItems: Understand current state with a check-in that is clear and measurable.
Record thoughts, context, and repeated signals without losing continuity.
See progress, trends, and active tasks with less noise.
Receive contextual support and crisis routes when needed.
therapistItems: Review real patients directly from Firestore.
Read the latest check-in, recent history, and case thoughts.
Observe recent missions and a basic clinical risk signal.
Get faster visibility into what happened between sessions.

science.title: A tool designed to support, not replace professional care.
science.description: NeuroQuest works as a support tool for follow-up and emotional understanding. It keeps clear limits and does not replace clinical care.
pillars: It accompanies you between sessions so you arrive with more clarity to each therapeutic space.
It organizes your evolution so you do not lose context over time.
It does not replace professional care or perform medical diagnosis.
science.safetyIntro: The public narrative also keeps explicit clinical limits.
safetyItems: It does not replace professional care or perform medical diagnosis.
It keeps clear messaging when a situation requires more support.
It preserves a calm and useful reading of the process.

finalCta.title: Start understanding your mind with more clarity, without doing it alone.
finalCta.description: A simple and continuous space to understand how you feel, record what you think, and follow your progress.

donation.title: Your support can open follow-up and care for more people affected by mental distress.
donation.description: Every donation helps sustain a platform built to support real processes with more clarity, continuity, and access. If you want to support this mission, leave your details and continue to the secure donation link.
donation.helper: Your contribution helps expand access, follow-up, and support for people who need mental-health care today.

footer.productDescription: Mental-health follow-up with clinical structure, therapeutic gamification, and contextual support for patients and therapists.
footer.safetyDescription: NeuroQuest is a support and follow-up tool. It does not replace professional treatment or medical instruction.
footer.disclaimer: No self-medication. No dose prescribing. Guidance proportional to risk.

exposure.title: Exposure Mode (ERP)
exposure.description: Engage in progressively challenging Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) missions. This is a core part of overcoming OCD and anxiety.

exposure.lockedDescription: Complete more missions to unlock these challenges.
exposure.mission1Description: Touch an object you consider mildly contaminated (like a doorknob) and don't wash your hands for 5 minutes.
exposure.mission2Description: Leave your house for a short walk (5-10 minutes) without performing your usual checking rituals for the door or stove.
exposure.missionCompleted1Description: Acknowledge an urge to perform a minor compulsion and consciously decide not to do it.
exposure.missionLocked1Description: After using the restroom, wait 15 minutes before washing your hands.

crisis.title: Crisis Support
crisis.description: If you are in crisis or immediate danger in Medellin or the surrounding metro area, use these emergency lines and mental health care centers. Your safety comes first.
crisis.disclaimer: NeuroQuest is a tool for support and skill-building, not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

medellin.title: Emergency Numbers – Medellín

mainEmergency.title: Serious Emergency → 123
mainEmergency.desc: Main line and fastest general option (police, ambulances, firefighters, etc.)

mentalCrisis.title: Urgent Mental Crisis → 123 or psychiatric clinic
mentalCrisis.desc: For a severe mental crisis (risk of suicide, aggression, psychosis), call 123 immediately. You can also go to the emergency room of any hospital (they will refer you to psychiatry).

grounding.title: Grounding Exercises
grounding.description: A collection of grounding techniques to help you anchor yourself in the present moment during times of distress.

ex1.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique
ex1.desc: Name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, 3 you can hear, 2 you can smell, and 1 you can taste. This brings you into the present moment.

ex2.title: Mindful Breathing
ex2.desc: Focus on the sensation of your breath entering and leaving your body. Don't change it, just observe it for a minute or two.

ex3.title: Hold a Comfort Object
ex3.desc: Pick up an object and focus on its texture, weight, and temperature. A smooth stone, a soft fabric, or a stress ball works well.

ex4.title: Describe Your Environment
ex4.desc: Look around and describe your surroundings in detail, either silently to yourself or out loud. What colors and shapes do you see?

ex5.title: Listen to a Song
ex5.desc: Put on a piece of music and really listen to it. Try to identify different instruments, the rhythm, and the melody.

ex6.title: Stretch Your Body
ex6.desc: Gently stretch your arms, legs, and neck. Pay attention to the feeling of your muscles lengthening and releasing tension.

ex1.title: Temperature Change
ex1.desc: Hold a piece of ice in your hand or splash your face with cold water. The sudden temperature shift can jolt your system back to the present.

ex2.title: Strong Scent
ex2.desc: Inhale a strong, pleasant scent like peppermint oil, coffee grounds, or a citrus peel. The intense aroma can cut through overwhelming thoughts.

ex3.title: Body Scan
ex3.desc: Mentally scan your body from your toes to your head, noticing any sensations of tension, warmth, or coolness without judgment.

ex4.title: Push Against a Wall
ex4.desc: Stand and firmly push your hands against a wall for 15-30 seconds. Focus on the feeling of resistance and the strength in your muscles.

ex5.title: The Categories Game
ex5.desc: Pick a category (like 'fruits', 'countries', or 'animals') and name as many items in that category as you can. This engages your thinking brain.

ex6.title: Eat Something Mindfully
ex6.desc: Take a small piece of food, like a raisin or a nut, and eat it very slowly. Notice its texture, taste, and the sensation of chewing.

ex7.title: Grip and Release
ex7.desc: Clench your fists as tightly as you can for 5 seconds, then release the tension. Repeat this with different muscle groups (feet, shoulders).

ex1.title: Intense Temperature (TIPP Skill)
ex1.desc: Holding your breath, dip your face into a bowl of cold water for 15-30 seconds. This activates the mammalian dive reflex to rapidly calm your system.

ex2.title: Intense Exercise
ex2.desc: Engage in a short burst of intense physical activity, like doing 15 jumping jacks, running in place, or shadow boxing. This helps burn off excess adrenaline.

ex3.title: Sour or Spicy Taste
ex3.desc: Bite into a lemon wedge or eat something with a strong, spicy flavor. The intense sensation provides a powerful sensory anchor.

ex4.title: Grounding Through Feet
ex4.desc: Stand barefoot and focus all your attention on the soles of your feet. Feel the texture of the floor and imagine roots growing from your feet into the earth.

ex5.title: Recite Something Verbally
ex5.desc: Say a familiar poem, a phone number, a song's lyrics, or just the alphabet out loud. The act of speaking and recalling engages your brain differently.

ex6.title: Anchor Phrase
ex6.desc: Repeat a simple, factual phrase to yourself, such as 'My name is [Your Name]. I am in [Your City]. It is [Day of the week].' This reorients you to reality.

ex7.title: Paired Muscle Relaxation
ex7.desc: Tense a muscle group (like your hands) while you breathe in, and then relax it completely as you breathe out. Move through different muscle groups.

challenges.title: Cognitive Distortion Challenge
challenges.description: Test your ability to identify common cognitive distortions. Read the thought and select the distortion that best fits.

questions.thought: I got a 'B' on the test. I'm a complete failure.
questions.explanation: This is Black-and-White Thinking because it sees a situation in only two categories (total success or complete failure) instead of on a spectrum.

questions.thought: My boss didn't say hello this morning. They must be mad at me.
questions.explanation: This is Mind Reading, a form of Jumping to Conclusions, because you're assuming you know what someone else is thinking without sufficient evidence.

questions.thought: I felt anxious during the presentation, so I'm sure everyone thought I was incompetent.
questions.explanation: This is Emotional Reasoning, where you assume that because you feel a certain way, it must be true. 'I feel it, therefore it must be true.'

questions.thought: If I fail this one project, my entire career will be ruined.
questions.explanation: This is Catastrophizing (or Magnification), where you expect the worst-case scenario to happen, blowing things out of proportion.

questions.thought: I made one mistake. Now I'll never get it right.
questions.explanation: This is Overgeneralization. You're taking a single negative event and turning it into a never-ending pattern of defeat.

medication.title: Medical Decision Support
medication.description: Clinical guidelines for pharmacological treatment based on diagnosis and severity. For professional use only.
medication.warningTitle: Professional Use Only - Not a Substitute for Medical Evaluation
medication.warningDescription: This system does not replace a professional medical evaluation. Doses must be defined and prescribed by a qualified physician. Do not self-medicate.

rules.1: Do not define doses automatically; use referential ranges according to clinical guidelines and validated by a physician.
rules.2: Requires professional medical validation to start, adjust, or suspend any treatment.
rules.3: Alert for suicide risk when starting antidepressants, especially in young people.
rules.4: Prioritize emergency care in case of crisis, risk of self-harm or harm to others.

checkIn.title: Mental Check-In
checkIn.subtitle: Evaluate your emotional state and receive a personalized mission to improve your mental well-being.
checkIn.subtitle_marketing: Discover how your mind is today and what to do to improve it step by step.
checkIn.importantText: This tool does not replace a medical or psychological diagnosis. It is a guide for self-assessment and orientation.
checkIn.alertText: Your response suggests significant discomfort or possible emotional risk. Seek professional support as soon as possible and contact a trusted person right now.
checkIn.disclaimerText: This tool provides general guidance based on self-reported symptoms. It does not substitute for a professional medical, psychiatric, or psychological diagnosis.
checkIn.defaultProfessionalNote: User completed a self-guided mental check-in.
checkIn.familySupportRecommendation: Share your current emotional state with a trusted family member.
checkIn.partnerSupportRecommendation: Ask your partner or support person for practical emotional support this week.
checkIn.workSupportRecommendation: Protect your work routine by keeping one focused block without multitasking.
checkIn.workReliefRecommendation: Reduce workload pressure and break complex tasks into smaller steps.
checkIn.studySupportRecommendation: Keep study sessions short and structured with a clear stop time.
checkIn.studyReliefRecommendation: Lower cognitive load and prioritize only essential study tasks today.
checkIn.exerciseRecommendation: Take a short walk or do light movement to regulate your nervous system.
checkIn.readingRecommendation: Review one grounding or CBT resource that helps you slow down and reframe thoughts.
checkIn.validationError: Please answer all questions before submitting.
checkIn.notesPlaceholder: Write how you feel, what happened today, or any observation...
checkIn.saveError: Could not save the check-in. Please try again.
checkIn.sessionError: No active user session was found.
checkIn.firestoreUnavailable: Firestore is not available in this session.
checkIn.reportClinicalSummary: Current result: {category}. Recorded score: {score}/{maxScore}. Suggested mission: {mission}.
checkIn.completeAllQuestions: Answer every question to see a real result before saving.

questions.text: Do you feel nervous, anxious, or on edge for no clear reason?
Have you lost interest or pleasure in activities you used to enjoy?
Do you have trouble sleeping or do you sleep too much?
Do you feel tired or lacking energy most of the time?
Do you find it hard to concentrate or make simple decisions?
Do you feel sad, empty, or hopeless?
Are you easily irritated or do you overreact?
Do you avoid social situations or important activities?
Do you have recurring negative thoughts about yourself?
Have you had thoughts of harming yourself or giving up?

moderate.description: CBT, gradual exposure, and emotional regulation.
tasks: Gradual exposure to an avoided situation

severe.description: Close monitoring, professional support, and specialized evaluation.
tasks: Talk to a trusted person today
Create a basic emotional safety plan

medical.title: Medical Support
medical.description: Basic clinical guidance based on your latest Mental Check-In stored in Firestore.
medical.reportDescription: Quick summary for clinical follow-up or discussion with a professional.
medical.reportClinicalSummaryEmpty: There is no saved clinical summary yet because no real check-in has been recorded.
medical.reportClinicalSummaryWithLevel: Latest recorded level: {level}. Suggested guidance category: {guidance}.
medical.disclaimerTitle: Not a substitute for medical evaluation
medical.disclaimerDescription: This view suggests a next step based on your recent severity. It does not generate automatic doses and does not replace medical, psychiatric, or psychological care.
medical.latestCheckInDescription: This uses the latest denormalized level stored in your user profile.
medical.noCheckInYet: There is no saved check-in yet to generate guidance.
medical.guidanceDescription: Content changes based on your latest recorded level.
medical.completeCheckInFirst: Complete your Mental Check-In first to unlock this clinical view.
medical.severeAlertDescription: Your latest level is severe. Prioritize professional help and use crisis support if there is risk or loss of control.

mild.title: Basic recommendations
mild.description: A mild level suggests close follow-up on habits and regulation.
items: Keep sleep, nutrition, and daily routine as stable as possible.
Use short breathing, grounding, or journaling exercises once or twice a day.
If distress increases or persists, schedule a professional evaluation.

moderate.title: Suggested intervention
moderate.description: A moderate level suggests increasing support and clinical follow-up.
items: Consider a psychology or psychiatry evaluation for structured follow-up.
Reduce isolation and define at least a minimal support network this week.
Monitor changes in sleep, anxiety, hopelessness, or self-harm thoughts.

severe.title: Clinical alert
severe.description: A severe level requires fast action and prioritizing safety.
items: Seek urgent professional support and do not stay alone if you feel loss of control.
Contact a trusted person and tell them you need immediate support.
If there is suicide risk, psychosis, or aggression, use Crisis Support now.

healthy.title: Preventive follow-up
healthy.description: A healthy level allows you to focus on prevention and monitoring.
items: Maintain protective habits and log important changes if they appear.
Keep periodic check-ins to detect early increases in distress.
Use the app to reinforce regulation, exposure, or observation when useful.
